interpretation: Geometry: one crown tooth + a ring sector (15.0° pitch wide), r ∈ [5.0, 11.0] mm, disk thickness 4.0 mm, tooth face height 2.8 mm.
Disk bottom clamped (z=0); tangential force 10.0 N applied uniformly to the tooth top face (z=6.8 mm), +y direction.
Peak vM in the root band = 55.83 MPa at the 10 N reference load.
Linear-scale to SIGMA_ALLOW = 30.0 MPa: F_allow = 5.4 N tooth tangential force.
T_safe(crown, 3D) = F_allow × PINION_RP / 1000 = 0.0161 N·m input-shaft torque.
Comparison: gear_fea.py (single-station 2D) gives T_safe = 0.0340 N·m; gear_fea_radial.py (radial 2D inner-edge bound) gives T_safe = 0.0131 N·m; this 3D solve gives T_safe = 0.0161 N·m.

CAVEATS: linear-elastic (no plasticity, no fracture); no neighbour-tooth load sharing (the sector is one pitch wide with free-ish ends, so this is conservative); uniform tangential traction on the tooth top (a real moving contact line is non-uniform); straight-flank tooth shape (will edge-load and gall in PA12-GF in a real mesh, not roll cleanly). The bench-grade ceiling remains the printed-coupon torque-to-failure test in motor/BENCH_TEST.md.
